title: The Kitchen Witch
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A truth-witch who can make people confess with her pies opens a bakery in a village where everyone has something to hide.
long_description:
Hazel Marrow has spent fifteen years wandering from town to town, using her gift as a truth-witch to expose corruption and move on. But when she arrives in the mist-shrouded village of Ashcombe, she decides to stay. She rents the old confectionery shop on Market Lane and begins baking pies with a peculiar magic: whoever eats one cannot tell a lie.

Her pies crack the village wide open. The mayor has been stealing from the village fund. The baker's boy is hiding from a crime he did not commit. A young mother's husband did not leave — he is buried in the mayor's cellar. As secrets spill like hot filling, Hazel must decide whether her gift is a weapon or a balm.

The Kitchen Witch is a cozy fantasy about community, courage, and the difference between forcing the truth and inviting it. Featuring warm bread, cold justice, and a love story that rises like good dough.
main_characters: Hazel Marrow — A wandering truth-witch who can make people tell the truth with her pies
Alderman Prickett — The corrupt mayor of Ashcombe
Finn (Tobias Flint) — The baker's boy, hiding from a false accusation of arson
Mrs. Hempstock — The village gossip and dry-goods shop owner
Constable Briggs — The tired, honest village constable
Old Sallow — A reclusive herb woman who lives at the edge of the woods
Nell — A young mother whose husband was murdered
Margaret Prickett — The mayor's wife, who finally leaves him
